name: Dehesas y veredas de Fregenal de la Sierra – circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around Fregenal de la Sierra offers routes through the rugged Sierra Morena foothills, characterized by varied topography and significant elevation changes. The region features extensive *dehesas* of holm oaks and cork oaks, providing open woodlands and rolling hills for scenic rides. Natural trails wind through lush pastures, allowing exploration of the area's diverse flora and fauna. This geographical setting provides a range of challenging mountain bike routes with varied gradients. Best mountain bike trails around Fregenal de la Sierra The most popular mountain bike trail is Dehesas y veredas de Fregenal de la Sierra – circular, a 40.3 miles (64.8 km) trail that takes 5 hours 30 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses traditional dehesa landscapes and natural paths. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cabeza de la Vaca por el camino de Valdezurrones y la Cañada Real Soriana – circular desde Segura de León, a moderate 22.3 miles (35.9 km) path. It follows historic drovers' roads and offers varied terrain. Local mountain bikers also love the Walled Enclosure (Alcazaba) – Church of Saint Michael the Archangel loop from Fregenal de la Sierra, a 45.8 miles (73.7 km) trail leading through natural areas and past historical landmarks, often completed in about 6 hours 21 minutes. Mountain biking around Fregenal de la Sierra is defined by Sierra Morena foothills, dehesas of holm oaks and cork oaks, and a network of natural trails. The routes offer options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Fregenal de la Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Fregenal de la Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Fregenal de la Sierra?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Fregenal de la Sierra, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes traverse the rugged Sierra Morena foothills and the scenic *dehesas* of holm and cork oaks, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Fregenal de la Sierra?
A: The terrain is quite varied, reflecting the region's position in the Sierra Morena foothills. You'll encounter rugged sections with significant elevation changes, as well as rolling hills through extensive *dehesas*. Many routes feature natural trails, some with steep climbs and good gravel surfaces, offering a mix of challenging and picturesque riding.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032231305/dehesas-y-veredas-de-fregenal-de-la-sierra-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas y veredas de Fregenal de la Sierra – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a 64.8 km loop that takes about 5 hours 30 minutes. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032247125/cabeza-de-la-vaca-por-el-camino-de-valdezurrones-y-la-canada-real-soriana-circular-desde-segura-de-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza de la Vaca por el camino de Valdezurrones y la Cañada Real Soriana – circular desde Segura de León&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.9 km long.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes in Fregenal de la Sierra?
A: If you're looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walled Enclosure (Alcazaba) – Church of Saint Michael the Archangel loop from Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 73.7 km trail, typically completed in about 6 hours 21 minutes. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jerez de los Caballeros loop from Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 60.9 km.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Absolutely. A popular moderate route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032247125/cabeza-de-la-vaca-por-el-camino-de-valdezurrones-y-la-canada-real-soriana-circular-desde-segura-de-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza de la Vaca por el camino de Valdezurrones y la Cañada Real Soriana – circular desde Segura de León&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35.9 km long and follows historic drovers' roads. Another good choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40002411" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de la Albuera loop from Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, a 31.3 km trail with moderate difficulty.

Q: What historical landmarks or attractions can I see near the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in history. You can explore the medieval &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5145360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Templar Castle of Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which uniquely houses an 18th-century bullring within its walls. Other nearby historical sites include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burguillos del Cerro Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5342239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Order of Santiago (Valencia del Ventoso)&lt;/a&gt;. The historic town center of Fregenal de la Sierra itself is a Property of Cultural Interest, with well-preserved architecture.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Fregenal de la Sierra?
A: The Sierra Morena region generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking during spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder. Summers can be quite hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if riding during this season. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to ride year-round, though some trails might be wetter after rain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Fregenal de la Sierra?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from the challenging Sierra Morena foothills to the serene *dehesas* of holm and cork oaks. The network of natural trails and the opportunity to combine rides with visits to historical landmarks are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching or wildlife spotting while mountain biking?
A: Yes, the natural environment around Fregenal de la Sierra is rich in biodiversity. The *dehesas* and lush pastures are home to Iberian pigs, and the broader Sierra Morena region supports wildlife such as deer and wild boar. There's also a dedicated birdwatching route in Fregenal de la Sierra, allowing you to spot over thirty species of birds, making it a great area for nature enthusiasts.

Q: What are the typical elevation gains on mountain bike trails in this region?
A: Given its location in the Sierra Morena foothills, trails around Fregenal de la Sierra can feature significant elevation gains. For instance, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walled Enclosure (Alcazaba) – Church of Saint Michael the Archangel loop from Fregenal de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; involves over 1370 meters of ascent. Even moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032247125/cabeza-de-la-vaca-por-el-camino-de-valdezurrones-y-la-canada-real-soriana-circular-desde-segura-de-leon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza de la Vaca por el camino de Valdezurrones y la Cañada Real Soriana – circular desde Segura de León&lt;/a&gt; can have over 600 meters of climbing, so be prepared for varied gradients.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through the traditional *dehesa* landscapes?
A: Many trails in the region wind through the characteristic *dehesas* of holm oaks and cork oaks. A prime example is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032231305/dehesas-y-veredas-de-fregenal-de-la-sierra-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dehesas y veredas de Fregenal de la Sierra – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which directly translates to 'Dehesas and paths of Fregenal de la Sierra'. These routes offer picturesque riding through open woodlands and pastures, providing a unique insight into the local agroforestry system.
tips: Between typical Extremaduran meadows, at this point we cross the railway line (Zafra-Huelva Line) built at the end of the 19th century. Nearby, we find the Dehesa Casablanca.
The Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora Santa María de los Remedios is a Baroque-style religious building dedicated to the patron saint of Fregenal de la Sierra, which began to be built in 1497.
Fun single track for mountain bike lovers. It is located in the Vereda from Jerez de los Caballeros to Bodonal de la Sierra.

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Remedies
Vereda de Jerez de los Caballeros to Bodonal de la Sierra
